evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Reconcile revenue data across systems of record (CRM, billing, ERP) and ensure a single source of truth for financial KPIs
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Reconciling revenue data across systems of record to ensure a single source of truth for financial KPIs is explicit governance/accuracy language, supporting a rigour classification.
evidence.domain_risk: single source of truth for financial KPIs
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Financial KPIs and a single source of truth for revenue reporting carry direct financial-reporting stakes, matching a high domain-risk classification.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build, maintain, and improve the revenue data models that power executive-level reporting and board-ready metrics
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Building, maintaining, and improving revenue data models that power executive-level reporting describes an established, actively growing function rather than a first build, matching mid maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: Reconcile revenue data across systems of record (CRM, billing, ERP) and ensure a single source of truth for financial KPIs
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The specific responsibility of reconciling revenue data across CRM, billing, and ERP systems reflects concrete hiring-manager detail combined with more general reporting language, matching mixed authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: dashboards and reports that translate complex revenue data into clear, actionable insights for sales, finance, and leadership
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Translating revenue data into insights for sales, finance, and leadership spans multiple stakeholder groups at once, supporting a mixed classification.
evidence.autonomy_level: Build, maintain, and improve the revenue data models that power executive-level reporting and board-ready metrics; Partner with sales operations and finance to understand changing business processes and reflect them accurately
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Building and improving revenue data models is scoped delivery work, while partnering with sales operations and finance to reflect changing business processes involves shaping how metrics are defined, matching mixed autonomy.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Revenue metrics are sourced from multiple systems, have historic complexity due to acquisitions and system migrations
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Revenue metrics sourced from multiple systems with historic complexity from acquisitions and migrations describes untangling and improving existing infrastructure rather than building new, matching fix_scale.
evidence.ai_role: Build, maintain, and improve the revenue data models that power executive-level reporting and board-ready metrics
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tools, frameworks, or AI-enabled infrastructure responsibilities mentioned.
evidence.testing_framing: Reconcile revenue data across systems of record (CRM, billing, ERP) and ensure a single source of truth for financial KPIs
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Verb 'ensure' paired with data reconciliation ownership indicates responsibility for data quality and integrity practices.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensure a single source of truth for financial KPIs; board-ready metrics; executive-level reporting
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Board-level financial reporting and trustworthiness of critical metrics dominate the framing over delivery or reliability concerns.
